title: Adaptive Proof Route Selection
larger_intended_outcome: Given a change in a host repository, help an agent choose the safest available proof route that matches the kind of confidence needed, without assuming Agentic Workspace source-repo commands or becoming the host repo's quality-policy owner.
non_goals: Do not make Agentic Workspace define what proof is sufficient for every host repo.
Do not require host repos to adopt a universal testing convention.
Do not hard-code Agentic Workspace source-repo commands into host proof selection.
Do not infer security, compliance, release, migration, or data-loss approval from discovered commands.
Do not treat persisted route hints as durable truth.

candidate_lanes.title: Advisory route hints from setup/adopt
candidate_lanes.outcome: Persist lifecycle-discovered proof route hints with provenance and require live confirmation before proof selection treats them as usable.
candidate_lanes.owner_surface: GitHub #901; implementation evidence in commit d9902b66
candidate_lanes.proof: Init/adopt writes route hints only when discovered, setup reports the surface read-only, and proof output separates confirmed from stale hints.

candidate_lanes.title: Explicit proof-route decision model
candidate_lanes.outcome: Expose proof intent, configured policy, learned hints, live capabilities, selected commands, unavailable routes, manual fallback, and execution evidence as distinct inspectable layers.
candidate_lanes.owner_surface: GitHub #901; implementation evidence in commit d9902b66
candidate_lanes.proof: Detailed proof output can reconstruct why each command or manual route was selected, skipped, replaced, or downgraded without conflating layers.

candidate_lanes.title: Host policy precedence
candidate_lanes.outcome: Make explicit host-configured proof profiles and unsafe-command constraints override learned hints and generic discovery.
candidate_lanes.owner_surface: GitHub #899; implementation evidence in slice commit Add host policy proof command blocks
candidate_lanes.proof: Tests show host-required proof wins, host-disallowed commands are blocked, and learned or discovered routes cannot silently replace policy.

candidate_lanes.title: Broader capability detectors
candidate_lanes.outcome: Add detectors for common repo ecosystems as capabilities and advisory hints, not as policy.
candidate_lanes.proof: Repos with Python, Rust, Go, Gradle/Maven, or alternate JS package-manager surfaces receive useful route candidates without unsafe assumptions.

candidate_lanes.title: Proof execution evidence feedback
candidate_lanes.outcome: Represent selected, run, passed, failed, skipped, unavailable, waived, and missing proof states distinctly.
candidate_lanes.proof: Closeout and proof reports distinguish route selection from execution results, and stale/unavailable routes can be downgraded.

candidate_lanes.title: Manual verification templates
candidate_lanes.outcome: Provide structured non-executable verification instructions by proof intent when no safe executable route exists.
candidate_lanes.owner_surface: GitHub #899; implementation evidence in slice commit Add manual proof fallback templates
candidate_lanes.proof: Proof output makes manual fallback explicit, lower-trust unless host policy says otherwise, and specific enough for agents to record evidence.

dependency_assumptions: Advisory route hints are the smallest useful first slice and should remain live-confirmed before use.
The explicit decision model should precede policy precedence, broader detectors, and execution-evidence feedback so later slices do not overload one command object.
Manual verification can be shaped in parallel with detector expansion after the decision model exposes proof intent clearly.
parallelization_assumptions: Manual fallback wording and broader detector expansion can proceed independently after proof intent is explicit.
Host policy precedence and proof execution evidence should remain separate slices because one is pre-selection authority and the other is post-selection fact capture.
proof_expectations: Each promoted lane records focused validation in its execplan.
Every lane must prove source-repo command leakage remains blocked.
Every lane must keep host policy separate from learned or discovered route assistance.
Compact proof output should stay action-oriented while detailed output remains explainable.
promotion_rule: Promote only lanes with a clear proof-intent layer, bounded write scope, explicit host-policy boundary, and tests across at least one real or fixture host-repo shape.
notes: This decomposition is the epic-level map for #899. It should guide slice order and issue creation, not collect execution history.
